steps.title: Identify the two structures separated by the blank
steps.content:
Left: “...have survived from roughly the sixteenth century to the present [day]” — an independent clause with a complete main verb and its modifiers.

Right: “being passed down through private libraries in the city of Timbuktu, Mali” — a gerund phrase (a noun phrase formed from the verb “pass down”).

steps.title: Apply the preposition-gerund rule
steps.content:
A preposition (here by) must connect directly to the gerund phrase that follows without intervening punctuation.

Choice A “day by” correctly reads as “present day by being passed down...” — the manuscripts survived by the method of being passed down.

Choices B, C, and
  • D break this connection: B improperly uses a semicolon before a phrase (not an independent clause)
  • C creates a sentence fragment with a period
  • D adds an unnecessary comma
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
